page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8i.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8475B processor running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It operates on the x86_64 architecture and does not utilize hyperthreading, ensuring dedicated execution resources per vCPU.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage for boot and data volumes. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. The hardware profile is optimized for compute-intensive workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation without the overhead of shared threads, offering a balanced resource density for processing-heavy tasks.

The qualitative cost efficiency of the ecs.c8i.xlarge is centered on its dedicated compute resources, making it suitable for workloads where CPU performance predictability is prioritized over large memory footprints. Appropriate workloads for this configuration include web serving, batch processing, microservices, and lightweight computational tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8i.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, running on the x86_64 architecture. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8475B processor at 2.7 GHz with 4 physical cores and no hyperthreading, this KVM-based virtual machine delivers dedicated CPU resources. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage and providing a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly suitable for compute-bound workloads that require predictable CPU performance without the need for massive memory allocations. It is well-suited for applications such as web serving, microservices, batch processing, and lightweight compilation tasks.
